input.description:
## File
- `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/swissarmyhammer-kanban/swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/defaults.rs:136-265`

## What
In `KanbanLookup::get()` and `KanbanLookup::list()`, the `"column"` arm uses `ctx.entity_context().await?.read("column", id)` and `e.to_json()`, while every other entity type (task, tag, actor, swimlane) uses the typed read methods (`ctx.read_tag()`, `ctx.read_actor()`, etc.) with manual `serde_json::to_value` + id injection.

## Why
This asymmetry means:
1. Column is the only entity using the new path in `KanbanLookup` -- if EntityContext has different serialization behavior, column will differ from the rest.
2. The JSON shape for column includes `entity_type` (from `Entity::to_json()`) but the other entities do not -- they only inject `id`. This could surprise downstream consumers.
3. As more entities migrate, each one needs manual updating. A single strategy would be cleaner.

## Suggestion
This is fine for now as a transitional step, but when other entities migrate to EntityContext, a unified approach (probably all going through EntityContext) will be needed.

- [ ] Document the asymmetry with a TODO comment
- [ ] Plan to unify once more entities migrate
- [ ] Verify column JSON shape is compatible with consumers expecting `entity_type`

#warning"
input.title: KanbanLookup asymmetry: column uses EntityContext, others use typed I/O

+**Done.** The asymmetry was already gone (all types use entity_context). Collapsed the duplicated per-type match arms into generic implementations using KNOWN_TYPES. ~110 lines of boilerplate reduced to ~25.

- [x] Boilerplate eliminated — single generic impl for get() and list()
- [x] JSON shape consistent — all use Entity::to_json()
